# Semantic Conventions for MCP Spans

**Status**: [Experimental](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/document-status/)

This document defines span conventions for Model Context Protocol (MCP)
operations. Each MCP JSON-RPC method produces one span on the client side and
one on the server side. The client span is the parent of the server span when
context propagation is available.

<!-- semconv mcp -->
<!-- NOTE: THIS TEXT IS AUTOGENERATED. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. -->
<!-- see templates/registry/markdown/snippet.md.j2 -->

## Common Attributes

The following attributes MUST be set on every MCP span, regardless of the
operation.

| Attribute | Type | Requirement Level | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mcp.server.name` | string | `Required` | Logical name of the MCP server as defined in the client's configuration. |
| `mcp.session.id` | string | `Required` | Unique identifier for the MCP session, established during `initialize`. |
| `mcp.request.id` | string | `Required` | The `id` field from the JSON-RPC request. |
| `mcp.server.transport` | string | `Required` | Transport mechanism. One of `stdio`, `streamable-http`, `sse`. |

The following attributes SHOULD be set when the information is available:

| Attribute | Type | Requirement Level | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mcp.server.version` | string | `Recommended` | Version of the MCP server, reported during `initialize`. |
| `mcp.agent.id` | string | `Recommended` | Identity of the agent making the call. |
| `mcp.agent.model` | string | `Recommended` | LLM model powering the agent (e.g., `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`). |
| `mcp.auth.method` | string | `Recommended` | Authentication method used. One of `none`, `api_key`, `oauth`, `iam`. |
| `network.transport` | string | `Recommended` | OSI transport layer (from general semconv). `pipe` for stdio, `tcp` for HTTP. |

## Status Mapping

MCP uses JSON-RPC error codes. Map them to span status as follows:

| JSON-RPC Error Code | Span Status |
|---|---|
| No error (result returned) | `OK` |
| `-32700` (Parse error) | `ERROR` |
| `-32600` (Invalid request) | `ERROR` |
| `-32601` (Method not found) | `ERROR` |
| `-32602` (Invalid params) | `ERROR` |
| `-32603` (Internal error) | `ERROR` |
| Application-defined errors | `ERROR` |

When the span status is `ERROR`, set the `otel.status_description` attribute to
the JSON-RPC error `message` field.

---

## `mcp.initialize`

Represents the MCP connection handshake. The client sends its capabilities and
the server responds with its own capabilities and server info.

**Span name**: `mcp.initialize`

**Span kind**: `CLIENT` on the caller, `SERVER` on the MCP server.

### Required Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.server.name` | string | Logical name of the MCP server. |
| `mcp.server.transport` | string | Transport used for this connection. |
| `mcp.request.id` | string | JSON-RPC request ID. |

### Optional Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.server.version` | string | Server version returned in the response. |
| `mcp.protocol.version` | string | Negotiated MCP protocol version (e.g., `2025-03-26`). |
| `mcp.client.name` | string | Client implementation name sent in the request. |
| `mcp.client.version` | string | Client implementation version. |
| `mcp.capabilities.tools` | boolean | Whether the server advertises tool support. |
| `mcp.capabilities.resources` | boolean | Whether the server advertises resource support. |
| `mcp.capabilities.prompts` | boolean | Whether the server advertises prompt support. |
| `mcp.capabilities.sampling` | boolean | Whether the server advertises sampling support. |

### Example

```
Span {
  name:       "mcp.initialize"
  kind:       CLIENT
  start_time: 2026-03-31T10:00:00.000Z
  end_time:   2026-03-31T10:00:00.150Z
  status:     OK
  attributes: {
    mcp.server.name:        "filesystem"
    mcp.server.transport:   "stdio"
    mcp.server.version:     "1.2.0"
    mcp.session.id:         "sess_a1b2c3d4"
    mcp.request.id:         "1"
    mcp.protocol.version:   "2025-03-26"
    mcp.capabilities.tools: true
  }
}
```

---

## `mcp.tools.list`

Represents a request to discover available tools from an MCP server.

**Span name**: `mcp.tools.list`

**Span kind**: `CLIENT` on the caller, `SERVER` on the MCP server.

### Required Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.server.name` | string | Logical name of the MCP server. |
| `mcp.session.id` | string | Session ID. |
| `mcp.request.id` | string | JSON-RPC request ID. |

### Optional Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.tools.count` | int | Number of tools returned in the response. |
| `mcp.cursor` | string | Pagination cursor, if paginated. |

### Example

```
Span {
  name:       "mcp.tools.list"
  kind:       CLIENT
  start_time: 2026-03-31T10:00:00.200Z
  end_time:   2026-03-31T10:00:00.220Z
  status:     OK
  attributes: {
    mcp.server.name:      "filesystem"
    mcp.server.transport: "stdio"
    mcp.session.id:       "sess_a1b2c3d4"
    mcp.request.id:       "2"
    mcp.tools.count:      5
  }
}
```

---

## `mcp.tools.call`

Represents an invocation of a specific tool on an MCP server. This is the most
critical span for observability -- it captures what tool was called, whether it
succeeded, how long it took, and optionally, the cost in tokens or currency.

**Span name**: `mcp.tools.call {mcp.tool.name}`

The span name MUST include the tool name as a suffix. For example:
`mcp.tools.call read_file`, `mcp.tools.call query_database`.

**Span kind**: `CLIENT` on the caller, `SERVER` on the MCP server.

### Required Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.server.name` | string | Logical name of the MCP server. |
| `mcp.session.id` | string | Session ID. |
| `mcp.request.id` | string | JSON-RPC request ID. |
| `mcp.tool.name` | string | Name of the tool being invoked. |

### Conditionally Required Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Condition | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mcp.tool.is_error` | boolean | Always | Whether the tool's response `isError` field is `true`. |

### Optional Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.tool.duration_ms` | int | Server-side execution time in milliseconds. |
| `mcp.content.type` | string | MIME-like type of the primary content block returned (`text`, `image`, `audio`). |
| `mcp.agent.id` | string | Identity of the calling agent. |
| `mcp.agent.model` | string | LLM model powering the agent. |
| `mcp.agent.delegator` | string | Parent agent ID in a delegation chain. |
| `mcp.cost.tokens_input` | int | Input tokens consumed by this tool call (if the tool wraps an LLM). |
| `mcp.cost.tokens_output` | int | Output tokens produced by this tool call. |
| `mcp.cost.estimated_usd` | float | Estimated monetary cost of this tool call in USD. |

### Events

The following span events MAY be recorded:

| Event Name | Description |
|---|---|
| `mcp.tool.input` | Records the tool input arguments (sanitized). |
| `mcp.tool.output` | Records a summary of the tool output (truncated if large). |
| `mcp.tool.progress` | Records a progress notification from the server. |

### Example

```
Span {
  name:       "mcp.tools.call read_file"
  kind:       CLIENT
  start_time: 2026-03-31T10:00:01.000Z
  end_time:   2026-03-31T10:00:01.350Z
  status:     OK
  attributes: {
    mcp.server.name:        "filesystem"
    mcp.server.transport:   "stdio"
    mcp.session.id:         "sess_a1b2c3d4"
    mcp.request.id:         "3"
    mcp.tool.name:          "read_file"
    mcp.tool.is_error:      false
    mcp.tool.duration_ms:   340
    mcp.content.type:       "text"
    mcp.agent.id:           "agent-7f3a"
    mcp.agent.model:        "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
  }
  events: [
    {
      name:       "mcp.tool.input"
      timestamp:  2026-03-31T10:00:01.000Z
      attributes: {
        mcp.tool.input.summary: '{"path": "/etc/hosts"}'
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Example (Error)

```
Span {
  name:       "mcp.tools.call query_database"
  kind:       CLIENT
  start_time: 2026-03-31T10:00:02.000Z
  end_time:   2026-03-31T10:00:02.500Z
  status:     ERROR
  attributes: {
    mcp.server.name:           "postgres-mcp"
    mcp.server.transport:      "streamable-http"
    mcp.session.id:            "sess_x9y8z7"
    mcp.request.id:            "7"
    mcp.tool.name:             "query_database"
    mcp.tool.is_error:         true
    mcp.tool.duration_ms:      480
    otel.status_description:   "relation \"users\" does not exist"
    mcp.agent.id:              "agent-7f3a"
    mcp.agent.delegator:       "orchestrator-001"
  }
}
```

---

## `mcp.resources.list`

Represents a request to discover available resources from an MCP server.

**Span name**: `mcp.resources.list`

**Span kind**: `CLIENT` on the caller, `SERVER` on the MCP server.

### Required Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.server.name` | string | Logical name of the MCP server. |
| `mcp.session.id` | string | Session ID. |
| `mcp.request.id` | string | JSON-RPC request ID. |

### Optional Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.resources.count` | int | Number of resources returned. |
| `mcp.cursor` | string | Pagination cursor. |

### Example

```
Span {
  name:       "mcp.resources.list"
  kind:       CLIENT
  start_time: 2026-03-31T10:00:03.000Z
  end_time:   2026-03-31T10:00:03.050Z
  status:     OK
  attributes: {
    mcp.server.name:      "knowledge-base"
    mcp.server.transport: "streamable-http"
    mcp.session.id:       "sess_k4l5m6"
    mcp.request.id:       "4"
    mcp.resources.count:  12
  }
}
```

---

## `mcp.resources.read`

Represents a request to read a specific resource from an MCP server.

**Span name**: `mcp.resources.read {uri}`

The span name SHOULD include a sanitized form of the resource URI. If the URI
contains sensitive data, the implementation SHOULD use a placeholder.

**Span kind**: `CLIENT` on the caller, `SERVER` on the MCP server.

### Required Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.server.name` | string | Logical name of the MCP server. |
| `mcp.session.id` | string | Session ID. |
| `mcp.request.id` | string | JSON-RPC request ID. |
| `mcp.resource.uri` | string | URI of the resource being read. |

### Optional Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.resource.name` | string | Human-readable name of the resource. |
| `mcp.resource.mime_type` | string | MIME type of the resource content. |
| `mcp.content.type` | string | Content block type (`text`, `blob`). |

### Example

```
Span {
  name:       "mcp.resources.read file:///project/config.yaml"
  kind:       CLIENT
  start_time: 2026-03-31T10:00:04.000Z
  end_time:   2026-03-31T10:00:04.080Z
  status:     OK
  attributes: {
    mcp.server.name:        "filesystem"
    mcp.server.transport:   "stdio"
    mcp.session.id:         "sess_a1b2c3d4"
    mcp.request.id:         "5"
    mcp.resource.uri:       "file:///project/config.yaml"
    mcp.resource.name:      "config.yaml"
    mcp.resource.mime_type: "text/yaml"
    mcp.content.type:       "text"
  }
}
```

---

## `mcp.prompts.get`

Represents a request to retrieve a prompt template from an MCP server.

**Span name**: `mcp.prompts.get {mcp.prompt.name}`

**Span kind**: `CLIENT` on the caller, `SERVER` on the MCP server.

### Required Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.server.name` | string | Logical name of the MCP server. |
| `mcp.session.id` | string | Session ID. |
| `mcp.request.id` | string | JSON-RPC request ID. |
| `mcp.prompt.name` | string | Name of the prompt being retrieved. |

### Optional Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.prompt.arguments` | string[] | Names of the arguments passed to the prompt template. |
| `mcp.prompt.messages_count` | int | Number of messages in the returned prompt. |

### Example

```
Span {
  name:       "mcp.prompts.get summarize_code"
  kind:       CLIENT
  start_time: 2026-03-31T10:00:05.000Z
  end_time:   2026-03-31T10:00:05.030Z
  status:     OK
  attributes: {
    mcp.server.name:            "coding-assistant"
    mcp.server.transport:       "stdio"
    mcp.session.id:             "sess_a1b2c3d4"
    mcp.request.id:             "6"
    mcp.prompt.name:            "summarize_code"
    mcp.prompt.arguments:       ["language", "style"]
    mcp.prompt.messages_count:  3
  }
}
```

---

## `mcp.sampling.complete`

Represents a server-initiated request for the client to perform LLM sampling
(completion). This is the reverse direction: the MCP server sends a
`sampling/createMessage` request to the client.

**Span name**: `mcp.sampling.complete`

**Span kind**: `SERVER` on the MCP server (it is the caller), `CLIENT` on the
agent/client (it is the responder).

### Required Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.server.name` | string | Logical name of the MCP server that initiated the sampling request. |
| `mcp.session.id` | string | Session ID. |
| `mcp.request.id` | string | JSON-RPC request ID. |

### Optional Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp.agent.model` | string | Model used for the completion. |
| `mcp.sampling.max_tokens` | int | Maximum tokens requested. |
| `mcp.sampling.stop_reason` | string | Reason the sampling stopped (`end_turn`, `max_tokens`, `stop_sequence`). |
| `mcp.cost.tokens_input` | int | Input tokens consumed. |
| `mcp.cost.tokens_output` | int | Output tokens produced. |
| `mcp.cost.estimated_usd` | float | Estimated cost in USD. |
| `mcp.content.type` | string | Content type of the response (`text`, `image`). |

### Example

```
Span {
  name:       "mcp.sampling.complete"
  kind:       SERVER
  start_time: 2026-03-31T10:00:06.000Z
  end_time:   2026-03-31T10:00:08.500Z
  status:     OK
  attributes: {
    mcp.server.name:          "code-analysis"
    mcp.server.transport:     "streamable-http"
    mcp.session.id:           "sess_p1q2r3"
    mcp.request.id:           "42"
    mcp.agent.model:          "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
    mcp.sampling.max_tokens:  4096
    mcp.sampling.stop_reason: "end_turn"
    mcp.cost.tokens_input:    1200
    mcp.cost.tokens_output:   850
    mcp.cost.estimated_usd:   0.0098
    mcp.content.type:         "text"
  }
}
```

---

## Context Propagation

MCP spans MUST propagate context using the W3C Trace Context standard
(`traceparent` / `tracestate`). The mechanism depends on the transport:

| Transport | Propagation Mechanism |
|---|---|
| `stdio` | Inject trace context as a `_meta.traceparent` field in the JSON-RPC params object. |
| `streamable-http` | Inject trace context in HTTP headers per the HTTP semantic conventions. |
| `sse` | Inject trace context in the initial HTTP request headers. |

When the MCP server receives a request with propagated context, the server-side
span MUST be a child of the client-side span.

## Span Hierarchy Example

A typical agent interaction produces the following span tree:

```
[agent.run]                                          (root span)
  [gen_ai.chat]                                      (LLM call that decides to use a tool)
  [mcp.tools.call read_file]         CLIENT          (agent calls MCP server)
    [mcp.tools.call read_file]       SERVER          (MCP server processes)
      [fs.read]                                      (server-internal I/O)
  [gen_ai.chat]                                      (LLM processes tool result)
  [mcp.tools.call query_database]    CLIENT
    [mcp.tools.call query_database]  SERVER
      [db.query]                                     (server-internal DB call)
```
